title: Curation never proposes deleting high-access, high-trust facts absent strong similarity
description: The similarity-dedup curator (dry-run and apply) must collapse genuine near-duplicates while leaving dissimilar high-trust, high-access facts untouched. Machinery-only scenario: no real-model layer.
adapted_from: Adapted from the mnemon memory-deep curation rubric (Apache-2.0, https://github.com/mnemon-dev/mnemon @ 41a9612)
contract_notes: Covers the built-in similarity-dedup tier today. The sibling curator-hygiene work may extend curation with LLM-review ops; the conservatism assertions here stay the contract for the deterministic tier.

facts.content: Hetzner runner keeps the self-hosted CI queue under two minutes.
Dashboard bundles are prebuilt before cargo package ships them.
Branch databases live under .tracedecay/branches keyed by branch name.
Demo note: the staging banner color is mauve for this project.
Demo note: the staging banner color is mauve in this project.

assertions.name: protected_facts_survive_apply
assertions.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memory_facts WHERE source = 'eval-protected'

assertions.name: duplicates_collapsed_after_apply
assertions.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memory_facts WHERE source = 'eval-dup'
